questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Paragraph Since gravitational field is conservative, we can conserve the total mechanical energy of the system of interacting particles. As the net force acting on a closed system is zero, we can conserve the linear momentum of the system. Since the gravitational forces between two interacting particles are actionreaction pair, the net torque experience by the system about any inertial point is zero. Hence, angular momentum of the system remains constant about any inertial point. A body of mass $m(\lt \lt M)$ is projected tangentially to earth's surface with a speed $v_0=\sqrt{\frac{4 G M}{3 R}}$
Question The maximum height attained by the body is
questions.solution: Apply conservation of energy and conservation of angular momentum.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Paragraph: Consider a star and two planet system. The star has mass M. The planet $A$ and $B$ have the same mass $m$, radius a and they revolve around the star in circular orbits of radius $r$ and $4 r$ respectively ( $M$ > > $\mathrm{m}, \mathrm{r}$ >> a). Planet A has intelligent life, and the people of this planet have achieved a very high degree of technological advance. They wish to shift a geostationary satellite of their own planet to a geostationary orbit of planet B. They achieve this through a series of high precision maneuvers in which the satellite never has to apply brakes and not a single joule of energy is wasted. $\mathrm{S}_1$ is a geostationary satellite of planet $A$ and $S_2$ is a geostationary satellite of planet B. Neglect interaction between $A$ and $B, S_1$ and $S_2, S_1$ and $\mathrm{B} \& \mathrm{~S}_2$ and A .
Question: The ratio of time of revolution of planet A and planet B about star is :
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Paragraph: A satellite is moving around earth (mass M, Radius $R$ ) in circular orbit of radius $2 R$ in equatorial plane.
Question: Find the area on the earth, this satellite covers for communication purpose in its one complete revolution w.r.t earth.
questions.solution:
All area of earth with be covered other than shaded area. $$ \begin{aligned} & \text { shaded area }=2\left[2 \pi \mathrm{R}^2(1-\cos \theta)\right] \\ & =4 \pi \mathrm{R}^2\left[1-\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}\right]=2 \pi \mathrm{R}^2[2-\sqrt{3}] \end{aligned} $$
area covered $4 \pi R^2-2 \pi R^2[2-\sqrt{3}]$ $$ =2 \pi R^2 \sqrt{3} $$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Paragraph: Consider a star and two planet system. The star has mass M. The planet $A$ and $B$ have the same mass $m$, radius a and they revolve around the star in circular orbits of radius $r$ and $4 r$ respectively ( $M$ > > $\mathrm{m}, \mathrm{r}$ >> a). Planet A has intelligent life, and the people of this planet have achieved a very high degree of technological advance. They wish to shift a geostationary satellite of their own planet to a geostationary orbit of planet B. They achieve this through a series of high precision maneuvers in which the satellite never has to apply brakes and not a single joule of energy is wasted. $\mathrm{S}_1$ is a geostationary satellite of planet $A$ and $S_2$ is a geostationary satellite of planet B. Neglect interaction between $A$ and $B, S_1$ and $S_2, S_1$ and $\mathrm{B} \& \mathrm{~S}_2$ and A .
Question: If the radius of the geostationary orbit in planet $A$ is given by $r_G=r\left(\frac{m}{M}\right)^{1 / 3}$, then the time in which the geostationary satellite will complete 1 revolution is I. 1 planet year = time in which planet revolves around the star II. 1 planet day = time in which planet revolves about its axis.
questions.solution:
For geostationary satellite, time period = 1 planet day (by def.) Let $\mathrm{T}=1$ planet day $T_0=1$ planet year
Now $T^2=\frac{4 \pi^2}{G m} r_G^3=\frac{4 \pi^2}{G m} r^3\left(\frac{m}{M}\right)$
$=\frac{4 \pi^2}{\mathrm{GM}} \mathrm{r}^3=\mathrm{T}_0^3$ or $\mathrm{T}=\mathrm{T}_0$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Paragraph: A satellite is moving around earth (mass M, Radius $R$ ) in circular orbit of radius $2 R$ in equatorial plane.
Question: A particle is projected from this satellite radially outward relative to satellite with speed $u$. Find the minimum value of $u$ so that particle escape from the gravitational force of earth. (Mass of earth >>> mass of satellite >>> mass of particle)
questions.solution:
$\frac{1}{2} m u_1^2-\frac{G M m}{2 R}=0$ $v_1=\sqrt{\frac{G M}{R}}$ $\frac{\mathrm{GM}}{2 \mathrm{R}}+\mathrm{u}^2=\frac{\mathrm{GM}}{\mathrm{R}}$
$v=\sqrt{\frac{\mathrm{GM}}{2 R}}$ $\therefore \text { Ans. (B) }$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
